title: Film and Soup
summary: The feature film “Jane Goodall the Hope.” “ Follow Goodall throughout her never-ending travels, capturing her relentless commitment and determination to spread a message of hope. author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Wedding Show
summary: This is a one-stop wedding shop, and what a stop it is. It’s an annual event held at the gorgeous Sullivan House, itself a premier wedding location whose hilltop grounds command some of the Island’s best views of both the Atlantic Ocean and New Harbor.
content:
This is a one-stop wedding shop, and what a stop it is. It’s an annual event held at the gorgeous Sullivan House, itself a premier wedding location whose hilltop grounds command some of the Island’s best views of both the Atlantic Ocean and New Harbor.

From noon - 3 pm, wedding vendors of every stripe – caterers, photographers, florists, even hair and makeup – assemble in a fun, relaxing Block Island atmosphere. You’ll enjoy live entertainment, great food, and drinks, and did we mention the view?
address: New Shoreham New Shoreham Washington County

author: Robert Lucier
title: Country Fest at Ballard’s
summary: Grab your ten-gallon hat and your snakeskin cowboy boots and head over to Ballard’s on Water St. for a toe-tapping’ foot-stompin', finger-snappin’ afternoon of all-American music, starting at noon until 7 pm.
content:
Grab your ten-gallon hat and your snakeskin cowboy boots and head over to Ballard’s on Water St. for a toe-tapping’ foot-stompin', finger-snappin’ afternoon of all-American music, starting at noon until 7 pm.

A great stop post parade or at any time on the 4th of July Holiday.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: 2020 Block Island Arts & Artisans Festival at the Narragansett Inn
summary: This island tradition takes place once a year on the Harbor Church lawn right in town. This three day festival is brimming with all things artistic; oil or water color paintings, jewelry, pottery, jewelry, fabrics, candles,photographs and other hand made pieces created by a very talented group of artists.

author: Analyn Alforque
title: Further Afield Fundraiser Lunch café during the Shopping Stroll (takeaway option too!)
summary: The BI School 10th Grade class will be serving up soup, cornbread and more! Come support theFurther Afield program, an annual environmental service and learning trip. This year the class will be volunteering at The Nature Conservancy’s Blowing Rocks Preserve in Florida.

author: Robert Lucier
title: American Legion Picnic
summary: One of the things many folks love about Block Island (in addition to the fabulous beaches, boating, fishing, and scenery!) is the way the simpler pleasures of summers past are still celebrated.
content:
One of the things many folks love about Block Island (in addition to the fabulous beaches, boating, fishing, and scenery!) is the way the simpler pleasures of summers past are still celebrated. 

It’s old-time Americana at its best, with All-American fare like hot dogs, hamburgers, salads, corn, baked beans and more. The price is old-time, too.

Just $10 for adults in advance (tickets will be sold at Farmer’s Markets and in front of BI Grocery,) and $12 at the door.

Just $5 for ages 7-12 and free for children under 7— and that’s for all you can eat!

The picnic starts at noon at the Legion’s Park location, so get there early while there’s still plenty of chow.

The American Legion is a veterans’ organization with the mission to serve veterans, military personnel and the communities where their posts are located. It’s nice to know that chowing down on picnic fare is helping a worthwhile organization and the people they serve.

meta.title: American Legion Picnic
meta.description: The American Legion Picnic will be on July 22, 2018.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Artist Talk Howard el-Yasin at Island Free Library
summary: Low Season Artist Projects is an artist-run residency program partnering with the Block Island Arts Council and the Old Town Inn. The residency promotes community interaction through lectures and workshops for island residents. It provides time and space for visiting artists to engage with the history, landscape and ecology of Block Island during the low season.
content:
Low Season Artist Projects is an artist-run residency program partnering with the Block Island Arts Council and the Old Town Inn. The residency promotes community interaction through lectures and workshops for island residents. It provides time and space for visiting artists to engage with the history, landscape and ecology of Block Island during the low season.

 

Free and open to all. 

 

Click on the image for more information

author: Austin Crawford
title: Run Around The Block 15K Road Race
content:
The race, begun by Vin McAloon and Paul Riker in 1975, the Run Around the Block 15K Road Race is one of the oldest races in Rhode Island as well as New England. The timed race is a hilly, winding run along mostly paved roads with a stretch of dirt road that appears twice during the route. It begins at Isaac's Corner near Fresh Pond and ends at Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion 

 

 

Online registration only @ itsyourrace.com 

                                                                till 1pm on September 97th

Bus transportation from Visitors Center to the start of the race 

From 11:30 to 12:30pm

Race starts at Isaac Corner and ends at the Fred Benson Town Beach

There will be music, swimming, showers,

Burgers, hot dogs, snacks & non-alcoholic beverages for sale 

at the Beach after the race

A box truck will be available at the start line for runners to stow their belongings in that will be taken to the end of the race. Items should be well marked with name & cell phone #. There will be limited room for bikes on a 1st come basis

 

 T shirt/number pick-up Saturday, 11-12:30pm at the race start

also Friday, 4 -6:30pm in front of the Chamber office at the Ferry dock

 

Proceeds to benefit “The Michael and Daniel Batchelder Memorial

Scholarship Fund” and The Block Island Community
address: Isaacs Corner New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mamma Mia!
summary: That's right- our Thursday movie invites us to bask in the sunny warmth of a Greek seaside inn, the upbeat beat of ABBA, and the stellar acting of Mery!
content:
That's right- our Thursday movie invites us to bask in the sunny warmth of a Greek seaside inn, the upbeat beat of ABBA, and the stellar acting of Mery!

Streep and a renowned cast. Come and join us for popcorn and the fun of sharing a movie- come and remember what sunny seaside days look like!

Light food is served starting at 12:30 PM and the movie rolls at 1 PM

author: BI Chamber
title: Free Electronics Recycling Drop-Off
summary: Free Electronics Recycling Drop-Off at New Shoreham Town Hall
content:
Free Electronics Recycling Drop-Off at New Shoreham Town Hall

We Accept:

  • Computers, laptops, servers, routers, mice, keyboards, plastic speakers, microphones
  • TVs, monitors and flat screens
  • Cell phones, cordless phones, telephones, PDA's, Ipods, tablets, chargers
  • Stereos, CD/DVD Players, console gaming systems, cassette players, remote controls
  • Toasters, microwaves, fans, vacuum cleaners, power tools AND MORE!

    *large appliances may incur $10 fee*

Visit indiecycle.blogspot.com for more information.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Run Around The Block 15K Road Race
summary: The race, begun by Vin McAloon and Paul Riker in 1975, the Run Around the Block 15K Road Race is one of the oldest races in Rhode Island as well as New England. The timed race is a hilly, winding run along mostly paved roads with a stretch of dirt road that appears twice during the route. It begins at Isaac's Corner near Fresh Pond and ends at Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion
content:
The race, begun by Vin McAloon and Paul Riker in 1975, the Run Around the Block 15K Road Race is one of the oldest races in Rhode Island as well as New England. The timed race is a hilly, winding run along mostly paved roads with a stretch of dirt road that appears twice during the route. It begins at Isaac's Corner near Fresh Pond and ends at Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion 

Walkers are welcome to participate and start one hour before the runners.  Runners begin at 1:30 and walkers at 12:30.

 

The race begins at Isaacs Corner.  A bus shuttle will be available for runners from the ferry landing to Isaacs Corner.

 

A van will be available to store and transport runners gear from the starting line to the finish line. The finish line will be at Fred Benson Town Beach where runners may use the showers.

 

A fundraiser cookout with DJ music will take place at the beach and is open to all.  The prizes for top finishers and first in each category will be awarded at the cookout.

 

The first 250 runners/walkers to register will receive a T-Shirt, backpack and finisher medal.

 

Proceeds from this race support the charitable actions of the Block Island Lions Club and Fund the Daniel and Michael Batchelder Memorial Scholarship Fund-a scholarship awarded  to graduates of the Block Island School. 

 

If you’d like to purchase a 50/50 raffle ticket for $20. You may purchase one from a race committee member or at McAloon's Restaurant.

All proceeds of the raffle support Block Island Lions Club charitable activities.
address: Isaacs Corner New Shoreham Washington County

author: Will Gasner
title: Annual Steak Fry
summary: Steak, corn on the cob, potato salad, pasta salad, watermelon, rolls, beer, and wine are standard fare at the annual fundraiser for the Block Island Fire Department.  Cost is $25/person.
content: Steak, corn on the cob, potato salad, pasta salad, watermelon, rolls, beer, and wine are standard fare at the annual fundraiser for the Block Island Fire Department.  Cost is $25/person.

meta.title: Annual Fire Department Steak Fry
meta.description: Annual Block Island Steak Fry to benefit the Block Island Fire Department.
